food_subgroup: Thai coconut milk red curry with beef. Red curry paste (น้ำพริกแกงเผ็ด) uses dried red chillies, lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime zest, shrimp paste, and garlic. Served as stew without rice.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for Thai red curry beef. Closest parent concept: coconut milk curry dish (FOODON:03315671 — curry dish). No specific Thai red curry beef entry exists in FoodOn as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Thai coconut milk red curry with beef and bamboo shoots
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0RCB used as closest parent for coconut milk-based curry dish with beef. No exact Thai red curry beef code exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Thai red curry beef dish. SNOMED CT does not catalogue prepared ethnic dishes at this specificity.

serving.description: 1 serving Thai red curry stew (~300g, no rice)
serving.natural_language_portion: One restaurant serving of Thai red curry beef stew is approximately 300g (no rice), comprising roughly 200ml coconut milk, 100g beef rump or sirloin, 50g bamboo shoots, and 2 tablespoons of red curry paste.

nutrient_source.title: Thai Food Composition Tables — Institute of Nutrition, Mahidol University (INMU)
nutrient_source.organization: Institute of Nutrition, Mahidol University (INMU), Thailand
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from INMU Thailand Food Composition Tables for Thai red curry beef (แกงเผ็ดเนื้อ). Per-100g values represent the composite stew (coconut milk + beef rump/sirloin + bamboo shoots + red curry paste) scaled to a 300g restaurant serving. INMU is an FAO/INFOODS-affiliated regional source and is an approved primary source under Project Veritas v2 Source Policy (FAO/INFOODS SEA category). Monounsaturated fat and polyunsaturated fat breakdown not separately reported in INMU tables for this dish; set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Dishes and Coconut Milk-Based Curries — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed analogous food estimation
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Thai red curry beef exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at ~30 (low) based on analogous coconut milk curry dishes with very low digestible carbohydrate content (~3.3g/100g, ~10g per 300g serving). The dominant macronutrients are fat (coconut milk lauric acid) and protein (beef), which strongly attenuate postprandial glucose response. GL per 300g serving = (30 × 10g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 3.0; rounded to 2.4 per INMU available carbohydrate estimate of 8g net. Estimation basis documented per Project Veritas v2 GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and shrimp paste in red curry paste
hypertension.guidance: One 300g serving provides approximately 876mg sodium (~38% of the 2300mg daily limit); individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced fish sauce, and balance with low-sodium foods throughout the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic load (GL ~2.4 per 300g serving, stew only)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The stew component alone has an extremely low glycemic load of approximately 2.4, making it well-suited for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes; the overall meal GL is determined almost entirely by any accompanying rice portion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk (~27.9g SFA per 300g serving, predominantly lauric acid C12:0)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 300g serving delivers approximately 27.9g saturated fat (>100% of the 20g daily limit for a 2000 kcal diet), primarily as lauric acid from coconut milk; individuals with cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia should consume this dish occasionally and in smaller portions, noting that lauric acid's cardiovascular impact remains debated in current literature.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Haem iron and zinc from beef rump/sirloin
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The beef component provides approximately 3.6mg haem iron and 7.5mg zinc per 300g serving, both important for managing anaemia of chronic disease in rheumatoid arthritis patients, though the high saturated fat content should be monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thai Red Curry with Beef (แกงเผ็ดเนื้อ, Gaeng Phet Nuea) is a Central Thai coconut milk stew made with beef rump or sirloin, red curry paste (น้ำพริกแกงเผ็ด), bamboo shoots, kaffir lime leaves, and red chillies. The red curry paste is distinguished from green curry by its use of dried red chillies rather than fresh green chillies, producing a deeper, more pungent heat. The dish is simmered in full-fat coconut milk, yielding a rich, aromatic, moderately spicy stew. A standard restaurant serving is approximately 300g stew without rice, providing 425 kcal, 21.9g protein, 32.1g fat (predominantly saturated lauric acid from coconut milk), and 9.9g carbohydrate. Sodium is approximately 876mg per serving from fish sauce and shrimp paste. Glycemic load is extremely low (~2.4) for the stew alone, making it suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when consumed without rice. The dish is widely available at Thai restaurants in Singapore including Jai Thai and Nakhon Kitchen. Nutritional data sourced from INMU Thailand Food Composition Tables.
common_user_queries: thai red curry vs green curry

thai red curry: แกงเผ็ดเนื้อ
red curry paste: น้ำพริกแกงเผ็ด

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from INMU Thailand Food Composition Tables, an FAO/INFOODS-affiliated regional primary source approved under Project Veritas v2. The dish (แกงเผ็ดเนื้อ) is a canonical Thai recipe with well-documented composition. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is estimated from analogous coconut milk curry dishes using the University of Sydney GI Database methodology, with estimation basis fully documented.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation (MUFA/PUFA breakdown) for Thai red curry beef stew is not separately reported in INMU Thailand Food Composition Tables as of 2026; the dominant fat fraction is saturated (lauric acid C12:0 from coconut milk, ~9.3g/100g). water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite stew dish is not reported in INMU tables. trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible/zero per INMU source for this dish). All core macros (protein_g 7.3, fat_g 10.7, carbohydrate_g 3.3) are non-zero and fully supported. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate based on coconut milk curry dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Thai red curry beef exists in the database. Halal status marked false as shrimp paste (kapi) is a standard ingredient in red curry paste; halal-certified versions may substitute or omit shrimp paste. Serving size of 300g represents a standard Thai restaurant portion (stew only, no rice). Per-serving values: energy ~425 kcal, protein ~21.9g, fat ~32.1g, saturated fat ~27.9g, carbohydrate ~9.9g, sodium ~876mg.
